description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a broad, symmetrical blade on each side, both featuring a slightly curved cutting edge and a pointed tip at the top. The blades are dark metallic with a weathered, almost blackened finish, and subtle red accents along the edges, giving it a menacing appearance. The central shaft is long, cylindrical, and appears to be made of metal, with a spiral pattern running its length, possibly for improved grip. The handle is straight and tapers slightly towards the end, with a consistent dark coloration and a matte finish. The axe head is securely mounted atop the handle, with no visible fasteners, suggesting a molded or integrated construction. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for heavy-duty use or as a display piece in an urban or fantasy-themed environment.
description: A double-headed battle axe with dark, weathered blades and a long, spiral-patterned metal handle. Red accents highlight the blade edges, giving it a striking and formidable look.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical blades with red-edged accents and the full length of the spiral-patterned handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the axe head and the cylindrical handle, with minimal blade width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the back of the double blades and the handle's spiral design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the axe and the straight, dark handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical metal handle with a spiral grip pattern, matte finish, and no visible curvature.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical blades with curved cutting edges, pointed top, and red accent lines along the edges; no beard or poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Modern molded or integrated mounting with no visible fasteners.
